1_CRITICAL_SECURITY._summary: MUST fix before ANY public or multi-user deployment. 6 critical + 6 high items.

CRIT_SEC_01.title: OS Command Injection via exec() with shell=true
CRIT_SEC_01.current_behavior: child_process.exec() with shell='/bin/bash' executes unsanitized AI-generated commands. Shell metacharacters (|;&$`) are checked but the check ENABLES shell path instead of blocking it.

changes.action: Replace exec() with execFile() for non-shell commands
changes.detail: At line 111, replace `await execAsync(command, { shell: '/bin/bash', ... })` with `await execFileAsync('/bin/bash', ['-c', command], { ... })`. Add command allowlist validation. Add `maxBuffer: 10 * 1024 * 1024` limit. Wrap in try/catch with sanitized error output.
changes.function: The anonymous async function handling tool execution around line 100-130

changes.action: Same pattern fix at line 218
changes.detail: Replace `await execAsync(command, { ... })` with `await execFileAsync('/bin/bash', ['-c', command], { ... })`. Add input validation: reject commands containing known dangerous patterns (rm -rf /, dd if=, mkfs, etc). Add timeout enforcement.

changes.action: Audit lines 550-572 for same pattern
changes.detail: If exec() is used with shell=true, apply same fix. Use execFile() with explicit arg arrays where possible.

CRIT_SEC_01.impact: Prevents arbitrary OS command execution from compromised LLM output or prompt injection
CRIT_SEC_01.testing: Create test cases: command with semicolons, pipes, backticks, $() substitution — all should be contained within single bash -c invocation

CRIT_SEC_02.title: Missing Authentication on 7 REST Endpoints
CRIT_SEC_02.current_behavior: checkAuth() exists in auth-utils.ts but is only called on /api/config and /api/skills routes. 7 routes are completely unprotected: /api/status, /api/sessions (GET), /api/sessions (POST), /api/sessions/:id (GET), /api/models (GET), /api/commands (GET). /api/health intentionally public.

changes.action: Add checkAuth guard to each unprotected handler
changes.detail: Import checkAuth from './routes/auth-utils'. Add `if (!checkAuth(req, res, config.gateway)) return;` as first line in each handler block for these routes:

keep_public.reason: Load balancer health checks must work without auth

CRIT_SEC_02.impact: Prevents unauthenticated access to full conversation history, session data, infrastructure details, and model configuration
CRIT_SEC_02.testing: For each protected endpoint: verify 401 without token, 200 with valid token, 401 with expired/invalid token

CRIT_SEC_03.title: Change Default Auth Mode from 'none' to 'api-key'
CRIT_SEC_03.current_behavior: Default auth mode is 'none', meaning all auth checks are bypassed unless user explicitly configures auth. Most users will never change this.

changes.action: Change default auth mode to 'api-key'
changes.detail: At line 39 (or wherever defaultConfig.gateway.auth.mode is set), change `mode: 'none'` to `mode: 'api-key'`. Add auto-generation of a random API key on first run if none exists: `key: crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex')`.

changes.action: Display generated API key during setup
changes.detail: After config is created, print the auto-generated API key to stdout with instructions on how to use it. Store in config.yaml under gateway.auth.key.

CRIT_SEC_03.impact: All new installations are secure by default. Eliminates the most common misconfiguration.
CRIT_SEC_03.testing: Fresh install should generate key, print it, and reject requests without it

CRIT_SEC_04.title: Installer Supply-Chain Integrity
CRIT_SEC_04.current_behavior: Installation via `curl | bash` from mutable main branch. No checksum, GPG signature, or version pinning. Also pulls Node.js setup script with sudo via curl|bash. Docker images use :latest tag.

changes.action: Pin to tagged release URL with SHA256 verification
changes.detail: Replace line 7 `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aaronboshart1/orionomega/main/scripts/install.sh | bash` with versioned URL: `https://github.com/aaronboshart1/orionomega/releases/download/v0.1.0/install.sh`. Add SHA256 checksum verification step before execution.

changes.action: Pin Docker image to digest instead of :latest
changes.detail: At lines 364-399, replace all `ghcr.io/vectorize-io/hindsight:latest` with `ghcr.io/vectorize-io/hindsight@sha256:<pinned_digest>`. Add a HINDSIGHT_VERSION variable at top of script for easy updates.

changes.action: Replace curl|bash Node.js install with package manager
changes.detail: At line 69, replace `curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_22.x | sudo -E bash -` with apt-based install or nvm-based install without piping to shell with sudo.

CRIT_SEC_04.impact: Eliminates supply-chain attack vector from DNS hijack, CDN compromise, or MITM
CRIT_SEC_04.testing: Run installer with tampered URL — should fail checksum. Run with correct URL — should succeed.

CRIT_SEC_05.title: WebSocket Token Exposure in URL Query Parameters
CRIT_SEC_05.current_behavior: Auth token passed as `?token=xxx` query parameter in WebSocket URL. Tokens appear in HTTP access logs, browser history, proxy logs, and Referer headers.

changes.action: Accept token via Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header during HTTP upgrade
changes.detail: At line 127, instead of `url.searchParams.get('token')`, read from `req.headers['sec-websocket-protocol']` or a custom header. The WebSocket upgrade request supports custom headers. Parse: `const token = req.headers['authorization']?.replace('Bearer ', '') ?? '';`

changes.action: Send token via WebSocket subprotocol or first message
changes.detail: At lines 16-21, remove token from URL construction. Instead, pass token as second arg to WebSocket constructor: `new WebSocket(url, ['bearer', token])` or send as first message after connection: `ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'auth', token }))`.

CRIT_SEC_05.impact: Prevents token leakage through logs and browser history
CRIT_SEC_05.testing: Connect with token in header — should authenticate. Verify no token appears in server access logs.

CRIT_SEC_06.title: Add Security Headers to Web Server (server.mjs)
CRIT_SEC_06.current_behavior: Gateway server (port 8000) has security headers via security-headers.ts, but the user-facing web server (port 5000, server.mjs) has NO security headers. No CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, or X-Content-Type-Options.

changes.action: Add security headers middleware to all responses
changes.detail: Add a middleware function before route handlers that sets on every response: `Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: blob:; connect-src 'self' ws: wss:; frame-ancestors 'none'`, `X-Frame-Options: DENY`, `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains` (when behind TLS), `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin`, `Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()`.

CRIT_SEC_06.impact: Prevents XSS, clickjacking, MIME sniffing, and other client-side attacks on the user-facing server
CRIT_SEC_06.testing: curl -I http://localhost:5000 — verify all headers present

CRIT_SEC_07.title: Enable TLS/HTTPS Support
CRIT_SEC_07.current_behavior: All traffic including auth tokens, API keys, and conversation history transmitted in plaintext HTTP.

changes.action: Add TLS config options to schema
changes.detail: Add to gateway config: `tls: { enabled: boolean, certPath: string, keyPath: string }`. Default to disabled but warn on startup if auth is enabled without TLS.

changes.action: Support HTTPS server creation
changes.detail: At line 741 (server creation), check if TLS config is present. If so, use `https.createServer({ cert, key }, handler)` instead of `http.createServer(handler)`. Add startup warning if auth.mode !== 'none' && !tls.enabled.

CRIT_SEC_07.impact: Protects all data in transit. Required for any network-accessible deployment.
CRIT_SEC_07.testing: Configure with self-signed cert, verify HTTPS works, verify HTTP redirect or refusal

CRIT_SEC_08.title: Token Revocation Mechanism
CRIT_SEC_08.current_behavior: Stolen API key is valid for 24h with no way to revoke it.

changes.action: Add revocation check during token validation
changes.detail: Maintain a revocation list in `~/.orionomega/revoked-tokens.json`. On each checkAuth call, check if the token's hash appears in the revocation list. Add `POST /api/auth/revoke` endpoint (protected, requires current valid token) that adds the token hash to the blocklist.

CRIT_SEC_08.impact: Allows immediate invalidation of compromised tokens
CRIT_SEC_08.testing: Issue token, use it successfully, revoke it, verify subsequent requests fail

CRIT_SEC_09.title: Restrict Hindsight Port Binding
CRIT_SEC_09.current_behavior: Hindsight Docker container binds port 8888 to 0.0.0.0, exposing the unauthenticated knowledge graph API to all network interfaces.

changes.action: Bind Hindsight to localhost only
changes.detail: At line 399 (docker run command), change port mapping from `-p 8888:8888` to `-p 127.0.0.1:8888:8888`.

changes.action: Same fix in programmatic Docker setup
changes.detail: Find docker run command around line 646, change port binding to 127.0.0.1:8888:8888.

CRIT_SEC_09.impact: Prevents remote access to unprotected memory/knowledge graph data
CRIT_SEC_09.testing: After fix, `curl http://<external_ip>:8888/health` should fail; `curl http://127.0.0.1:8888/health` should succeed

CRIT_SEC_10.title: API Key Secure Storage
CRIT_SEC_10.current_behavior: API key stored in plaintext in config.yaml, readable by same-user processes, visible in process listings.

changes.action: Support env-var-only key storage with config reference
changes.detail: Allow config.yaml to reference environment variables: `anthropic_api_key: ${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}`. Resolve env vars during config loading. Document Vault/Keychain/AWS Secrets Manager integration patterns. Remove any code that writes the API key directly to config.yaml during setup.

CRIT_SEC_10.testing: Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var, verify config loads it, verify key not written to disk

CRIT_SEC_11.title: Add Vary: Origin Header to CORS
CRIT_SEC_11.current_behavior: CORS headers set without Vary: Origin, causing cache poisoning risk with CDN/proxy.

changes.action: Add Vary header in setCorsHeaders function
changes.detail: In the setCorsHeaders function around line 529-537, add `res.setHeader('Vary', 'Origin');` after setting Access-Control-Allow-Origin.

CRIT_SEC_11.testing: Verify Vary: Origin header present in responses with different Origin request headers

CRIT_SEC_12.title: Sanitize Audit Log Tool Arguments
CRIT_SEC_12.current_behavior: Audit log records tool arguments including potentially sensitive data (API keys, passwords, tokens).

changes.action: Redact sensitive patterns before logging
changes.detail: At lines 28-35, before writing tool arguments to the audit log, run them through a redaction function that replaces patterns matching API keys (sk-ant-*, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=*), passwords, tokens, and authorization headers with '[REDACTED]'.

CRIT_SEC_12.impact: Prevents credential leakage through audit logs
CRIT_SEC_12.testing: Trigger tool call with API key in args, verify audit log contains [REDACTED]

2_MEMORY_SYSTEM._summary: Hindsight memory improvements for token efficiency, recall quality, and unbounded growth prevention. 11 items.

MEM_01.title: Fix Memory Injection Token Waste — Use System Parameter Instead of Synthetic Messages
MEM_01.current_behavior: Memory injected as two synthetic messages: `{ role: 'user', content: '[PRIOR CONTEXT]\n...' }` + `{ role: 'assistant', content: 'Understood. I have the prior context.' }`. This wastes tokens (synthetic ack), creates fake turn boundaries, and prevents Anthropic prompt caching.

changes.action: Move prior context to system parameter with cache_control
changes.detail: At lines 1112-1114, instead of pushing two messages, append the prior context to the system prompt: `system: [{ type: 'text', text: baseSystemPrompt }, { type: 'text', text: assembled.priorContext, cache_control: { type: 'ephemeral' } }]`. Remove the synthetic 'Understood' assistant message entirely. This enables Anthropic prompt caching (90% cheaper on cache reads) and eliminates the fake turn boundary.

MEM_01.impact: 30-50% token cost reduction on memory-heavy conversations. Enables prompt caching.
MEM_01.testing: Compare token costs before/after on same conversation. Verify LLM still uses prior context correctly.

MEM_02.title: Replace Trigram Similarity with Lightweight Semantic Scoring
MEM_02.current_behavior: Client-side relevance scoring uses trigram Jaccard + keyword overlap. This is character-level only — synonym matching fails ('fix' vs 'resolve', 'auth' vs 'authentication'). Paraphrased content scores near zero.

changes.action: Add synonym expansion and BM25-lite scoring
changes.detail: In `computeClientRelevance()` at line 67: (1) Add a synonym map: { fix: ['resolve','repair','patch'], auth: ['authentication','login','credentials'], ... } — expand query terms before matching. (2) Replace raw keyword overlap with BM25-lite: `score = sum(idf(term) * tf(term, doc) * (k1+1) / (tf + k1*(1-b+b*dl/avgdl)))` where k1=1.2, b=0.75. (3) Keep trigram as tiebreaker (0.2 weight) not primary signal. (4) Add word stemming (porter stemmer, ~50 lines).

MEM_02.impact: 2-3x improvement in recall relevance for paraphrased and synonym-heavy queries
MEM_02.testing: Test: 'fix authentication bug' should match memory about 'resolve login issue'. 'refactored auth' should match 'rewrote authentication module'.

MEM_03.title: Reduce Per-Turn HTTP Request Amplification
MEM_03.current_behavior: recallWithTemporalDiversity fires 4 parallel HTTP requests per bank (primary + 3 temporal windows). With N banks, each user message triggers 4N HTTP requests. 5 banks = 20 round-trips per message.

changes.action: Batch recalls and add response caching
changes.detail: In `recallWithTemporalDiversity()` at line 335: (1) Add a 30-second in-memory LRU cache (max 100 entries) keyed on `${bankId}:${queryHash}:${temporalWindow}`. Skip HTTP call if cache hit. (2) For multi-bank recalls, batch into single HTTP request if Hindsight API supports it, or serialize with connection pooling. (3) Add config option `memory.maxBanksPerRecall: 3` to cap bank fan-out. (4) Skip temporal diversity requests for 'low' budget tier — only fire primary recall.

MEM_03.impact: 50-80% reduction in per-turn HTTP requests. Sub-100ms recall latency for cached queries.
MEM_03.testing: Measure request count and latency before/after. Verify cache invalidation on new retentions.

MEM_04.title: Add Memory Expiration and Garbage Collection
MEM_04.current_behavior: No TTL, no expiration, no cleanup. retainOne() only appends. Banks grow unbounded. At 10K+ memories, trigram dedup checks become O(n).

changes.action: Add memory lifecycle management
changes.detail: Add new methods: `pruneBank(bankId, { maxAge: 90, maxCount: 5000, minRelevance: 0.2 })` that calls Hindsight API to delete old/low-relevance memories. Add `compactBank(bankId)` that merges similar memories. Schedule weekly via setInterval or external cron.

changes.action: Add retention limits
changes.detail: Before calling retainOne(), check bank size. If bank exceeds maxCount (configurable, default 5000), run pruneBank() first. Add relevance decay: memories older than 90 days get score multiplied by 0.7.

MEM_04.impact: Prevents unbounded storage growth and query degradation
MEM_04.testing: Seed bank with 10K memories, verify prune reduces to target count, verify old low-relevance memories removed first

MEM_05.title: Improve Token Count Estimation
MEM_05.current_behavior: `Math.ceil(text.length / 4)` used everywhere. Undercounts by 30-50% for code-heavy content (tokens are shorter in code).

changes.action: Use content-aware token estimation
changes.detail: Replace `Math.ceil(text.length / 4)` at line 99 with a function that detects content type: for code (detected by backtick blocks, indentation patterns), use `text.length / 3`; for prose, use `text.length / 4.5`; for mixed, use `text.length / 3.7`. Extract to shared utility `estimateTokens(text: string): number` in a new file `packages/core/src/utils/tokens.ts`.

changes.action: Use same shared estimateTokens utility at line 88
Use same shared estimateTokens utility at line 331

MEM_05.impact: 10-20% more accurate token budgeting, prevents context window overflow
MEM_05.testing: Compare estimateTokens output against actual tokenizer (tiktoken) for 100 sample messages

MEM_06.title: Fix Bank Slug Collisions
MEM_06.current_behavior: Bank slugs derived from task descriptions can collide. 'Build landing page for Acme' and 'Update landing page Acme' both generate `project-landing-page-acme`.

changes.action: Add collision detection and disambiguation
changes.detail: At lines 101-110, after generating slug: (1) Check if bank with slug already exists via listBanks(). (2) If exists, append short hash of full task description: `project-landing-page-acme-a3f2`. (3) Add `bankDescription` metadata to bank creation for disambiguation.

MEM_06.testing: Create two tasks with similar names, verify separate banks created

MEM_07.title: Add LLM-Based Quality Scoring Option for High-Value Memories
MEM_07.current_behavior: Quality scoring is regex-only. Pattern matching for keywords like 'decision', 'architecture', 'blocker'. No semantic understanding of memory importance.

changes.action: Add optional LLM quality evaluation for borderline memories
changes.detail: At lines 84-125, after regex scoring: if score is between 0.25-0.45 (borderline), optionally invoke cheap model (claude-haiku) with prompt: 'Rate the importance of this message for long-term project memory on a scale of 0-1: [content]'. Use LLM score as tiebreaker. Gate behind config flag `memory.llmQualityScoring: boolean` (default false). Cache LLM scores.

MEM_07.impact: 10-30% improvement in retention quality for borderline memories
MEM_07.testing: Compare retention decisions with/without LLM scoring on 50 sample messages

MEM_08.title: Session Anchor Cleanup
MEM_08.current_behavior: Session anchors stored with context 'session_anchor' but only latest recalled. Old anchors accumulate forever.

changes.action: Prune old session anchors
changes.detail: At line 128 where new anchor is stored, also delete anchors older than 30 days. Add a cleanup method `pruneOldAnchors(maxAge: number = 30)` that lists all session_anchor entries and deletes those beyond maxAge.

MEM_08.impact: Prevents anchor accumulation consuming storage and slowing queries
MEM_08.testing: Create 100 anchors over simulated 60-day period, verify prune keeps only last 30 days

MEM_09.title: Improve Query Classification Beyond Regex
MEM_09.current_behavior: Query type classification (factual, procedural, decision, etc.) uses regex patterns only.

changes.action: Add intent keywords and fallback classification
changes.detail: Expand regex patterns to cover more query types. Add keyword-to-intent mapping. For unclassified queries, default to 'general' with broader recall strategy instead of narrow pattern match.

MEM_09.impact: Better recall strategy selection for edge-case queries
MEM_09.testing: Test with 50 diverse queries, measure classification accuracy

MEM_10.title: Add Memory Usage Metrics Endpoint
MEM_10.current_behavior: No visibility into memory system health: bank sizes, recall latencies, retention rates, cache hit rates.

changes.action: Add metrics collection
changes.detail: Add internal counters: recallCount, retainCount, cacheHits, cacheMisses, avgRecallLatencyMs, totalMemoriesStored. Expose via `getMetrics()` method.

changes.action: Include memory metrics in status endpoint
changes.detail: Add hindsight metrics to /api/status response: `memory: { banks: N, totalMemories: N, avgRecallMs: N, cacheHitRate: N }`.

MEM_10.impact: Operational visibility into memory system performance
MEM_10.testing: Verify metrics increment correctly after recall/retain operations

MEM_11.title: Hot Window Persistence Robustness
MEM_11.current_behavior: Hot window (20 messages) persisted as JSON. If write fails, context is lost silently.

changes.action: Add write-ahead logging and error handling
changes.detail: At lines 707-751, write to temp file first then atomic rename. Add try/catch with error logging. On load failure, log warning and start with empty window instead of crashing.

MEM_11.impact: Prevents silent context loss on disk errors
MEM_11.testing: Simulate disk full scenario, verify graceful degradation

3_WEBUI_FEATURES._summary: Message retention, activity feeds, token tracking, run details, DAG visualization. 12 items.

WEBUI_FEAT_01.title: Track and Display Conversational Token Usage
WEBUI_FEAT_01.current_behavior: DAG workflow tokens tracked via dag-complete metadata, but non-DAG conversational tokens completely untracked. Users have zero cost visibility for regular chat.

changes.action: Emit token usage events for conversational messages
changes.detail: After each Claude API response, broadcast a `token_usage` WebSocket event: `{ type: 'token_usage', data: { inputTokens, outputTokens, cacheReadTokens, cacheCreationTokens, model, costUsd } }`. Extract usage from Anthropic API response headers/body.

changes.action: Handle token_usage events
changes.detail: Add handler for 'token_usage' message type. Update chat store with cumulative session totals.

changes.action: Add token tracking state
changes.detail: Add to store: `sessionTokens: { input: number, output: number, cacheRead: number, totalCostUsd: number }`. Add `addTokenUsage(usage)` action. Display in UI header or footer.

WEBUI_FEAT_01.impact: Users can monitor and control API spending in real-time
WEBUI_FEAT_01.testing: Send chat message, verify token count displayed and updates after each response

WEBUI_FEAT_02.title: Fix ActivityFeed React Key Collisions
WEBUI_FEAT_02.current_behavior: Keys use `${timestamp}-${index}` which collides when parallel workers emit events at the same timestamp.

changes.action: Use unique IDs for React keys
changes.detail: At line 148, replace `key={`${event.timestamp}-${index}`}` with `key={event.id ?? `${event.timestamp}-${event.workerId ?? 'system'}-${index}`}`. Better: add `id: string` field to activity events using crypto.randomUUID() at creation time.

WEBUI_FEAT_02.impact: Prevents rendering bugs and state corruption with parallel workers
WEBUI_FEAT_02.testing: Run 5-node DAG, verify no duplicate key warnings in console

WEBUI_FEAT_03.title: Fix DAGVisualization ReactFlow State Drift
WEBUI_FEAT_03.current_behavior: React.memo and ReactFlow internal state desync. Layout recomputes every render.

changes.action: Use useNodesState/useEdgesState hooks and stable layout
changes.detail: At lines 88-94: (1) Replace manual node/edge state with ReactFlow's `useNodesState()` and `useEdgesState()` hooks. (2) Memoize layout computation with `useMemo()` keyed on node count and edge list hash. (3) Only recompute layout when graph topology changes, not on status updates. (4) Use `onNodesChange` and `onEdgesChange` callbacks for ReactFlow-driven updates.

WEBUI_FEAT_03.impact: Eliminates visual flicker and state desyncs in DAG visualization
WEBUI_FEAT_03.testing: Run multi-node DAG, verify smooth status transitions without layout jumps

WEBUI_FEAT_04.title: Add DAG Edge Labels and MiniMap
WEBUI_FEAT_04.current_behavior: No edge labels showing data flow. No MiniMap for navigation in large graphs. Fixed spacing doesn't scale.

changes.action: Add edge labels and MiniMap component
changes.detail: Import `MiniMap` from '@xyflow/react'. Add `<MiniMap />` component inside ReactFlow. For edges, add `label` property showing dependency relationship. Make node spacing dynamic based on node count: `spacing = Math.max(200, 350 - nodeCount * 10)`.

WEBUI_FEAT_04.impact: Better navigation and understanding of complex DAG workflows
WEBUI_FEAT_04.testing: Create 10+ node DAG, verify MiniMap shows overview, edges have labels

WEBUI_FEAT_05.title: Config-Driven Token Pricing
WEBUI_FEAT_05.current_behavior: Pricing hardcoded with 'June 2025' Anthropic rates. No config-driven updates. Cache tokens displayed but not separately costed (cache reads are 90% cheaper).

changes.action: Move pricing to config and add cache-aware costing
changes.detail: Extract pricing constants to a separate `packages/web/src/lib/pricing.ts` file. Load pricing from gateway config (add `GET /api/pricing` endpoint or include in `/api/config`). Calculate cache read tokens at 10% of standard input cost. Display breakdown: `Input: $X.XX | Cache Read: $X.XX (90% savings) | Output: $X.XX | Total: $X.XX`.

WEBUI_FEAT_05.impact: Accurate cost reporting as model prices change. Users see cache savings.
WEBUI_FEAT_05.testing: Verify pricing updates when config changes. Verify cache savings displayed correctly.

WEBUI_FEAT_06.title: Add ActivityFeed Filtering
WEBUI_FEAT_06.current_behavior: ActivityFeed has zero filtering capability, unlike MemoryFeed which has comprehensive type toggles.

changes.action: Add filter controls matching MemoryFeed pattern
changes.detail: Add filter bar at top of ActivityFeed with: (1) Worker filter dropdown (show events from specific worker). (2) Event type toggles: status, tool_call, error, memory, dag. (3) Text search field for event content. Model on the existing MemoryFeed.tsx filter implementation (packages/web/src/components/orchestration/MemoryFeed.tsx).

WEBUI_FEAT_06.impact: Users can focus on relevant events during complex workflows
WEBUI_FEAT_06.testing: Run workflow, filter by worker, verify only that worker's events shown

WEBUI_FEAT_07.title: Run History Page with Persistent Storage
WEBUI_FEAT_07.current_behavior: No dedicated run history page. Past runs only visible inline in chat. GraphState frozen after completion; node details unavailable after tab close.

changes.action: Persist completed run data
changes.detail: Add `completedRuns: RunSummary[]` to orchestration store with Zustand persist middleware. Store: runId, timestamp, nodeStatuses, totalCost, duration, workerOutputPaths. Cap at 50 most recent runs.

changes.action: Add 'History' tab to orchestration pane
changes.detail: Add a third tab 'History' alongside existing tabs. Show list of completed runs with: date, duration, cost, node count, status. Click to expand and view DAG visualization + worker details for that run.

WEBUI_FEAT_07.impact: Users can review and compare past workflow executions
WEBUI_FEAT_07.testing: Run 3 workflows, close tab, reopen, verify all 3 visible in history

WEBUI_FEAT_08.title: Cumulative Session Cost Display
WEBUI_FEAT_08.current_behavior: Individual run costs shown in RunSummaryCard but no cumulative session total.

changes.action: Track cumulative cost
changes.detail: Add `sessionCost: number` to chat store. Increment on each token_usage event and dag-complete event. Persist with Zustand persist middleware.

changes.action: Display cumulative cost in header
changes.detail: Add small cost badge in chat pane header: `Session: $X.XX`. Update in real-time as messages are sent/received.

WEBUI_FEAT_08.impact: Users can monitor total spend across a session
WEBUI_FEAT_08.testing: Send multiple messages and run DAG, verify cumulative total matches sum of individual costs

WEBUI_FEAT_09.title: Virtualize ActivityFeed for Large Event Lists
WEBUI_FEAT_09.current_behavior: 1000-event ring buffer rendered as full DOM without virtualization. Causes jank with many events.

changes.action: Add virtual scrolling with react-virtuoso
changes.detail: Replace the mapped event list with `<Virtuoso data={filteredEvents} itemContent={(index, event) => <ActivityItem event={event} />} />`. Install `react-virtuoso` package. This renders only visible items in the viewport.

WEBUI_FEAT_09.impact: Smooth scrolling regardless of event count
WEBUI_FEAT_09.testing: Generate 5000 events, verify smooth scroll performance (60fps)

WEBUI_FEAT_10.title: Improve formatTokens Precision

changes.action: Add tooltip with full precision
changes.detail: Keep compact display (1.2M) but add `title` attribute with full number: `1.2M`. Use Intl.NumberFormat for locale-aware formatting.

WEBUI_FEAT_10.impact: Users can see exact token counts on hover
WEBUI_FEAT_10.testing: Hover over token count, verify tooltip shows full number

WEBUI_FEAT_11.title: WorkerDetail Data Persistence
WEBUI_FEAT_11.current_behavior: WorkerDetail reasoning/tool tabs lose data on refresh.

changes.action: Persist worker detail data in orchestration store
changes.detail: Store worker reasoning and tool call history in orchestration store (already persisted). On component mount, load from store instead of only relying on WebSocket events.

WEBUI_FEAT_11.testing: View worker detail, refresh page, verify data still visible

WEBUI_FEAT_12.title: Handle proOptions License Requirement
WEBUI_FEAT_12.current_behavior: `proOptions={{ hideAttribution: true }}` may require ReactFlow Pro license.

changes.action: Remove proOptions or acquire license
changes.detail: If no Pro license: remove `proOptions={{ hideAttribution: true }}` to comply with ReactFlow open-source license. The ReactFlow attribution watermark is small and non-intrusive.

WEBUI_FEAT_12.testing: Verify ReactFlow renders correctly with attribution visible

4_WEBUI_UX._summary: UI/UX improvements following design best practices. 11 items.

UX_01.title: WCAG AA Color Contrast Fix
UX_01.current_behavior: `text-zinc-600` on `bg-zinc-900` = 2.8:1 contrast ratio (below WCAG AA 4.5:1 minimum).

changes.action: Replace text-zinc-600 with text-zinc-400 globally
changes.detail: Search all .tsx files for `text-zinc-600` and replace with `text-zinc-400` (contrast ratio 5.3:1, meets AA). For secondary text that needs to be dimmer, use `text-zinc-500` (4.1:1) with `font-size: 0.875rem` (AA for large text).

UX_01.impact: Meets WCAG AA accessibility standards. Better readability for all users.
UX_01.testing: Use axe-core browser extension to verify no contrast violations

UX_02.title: Add Keyboard Navigation and ARIA Labels
UX_02.current_behavior: No skip-to-content link. Missing aria-label on interactive elements. No keyboard navigation in TabGroup (Arrow keys). Color-only status indicators.

changes.action: Add role='tablist', role='tab', aria-selected, arrow key navigation
changes.detail: Add `role='tablist'` to container, `role='tab'` + `aria-selected` + `tabIndex` to each tab button. Handle ArrowLeft/ArrowRight for tab switching. Handle Home/End for first/last tab.

changes.action: Add aria-label and role='log'
changes.detail: Add `role='log'` and `aria-label='Activity feed'` to the feed container. Add `aria-live='polite'` for new events.

changes.action: Add aria-label to input and buttons
changes.detail: Add `aria-label='Message input'` to textarea. Add `aria-label='Send message'` to send button. Add `aria-label='Attach file'` to file attachment button.

UX_02.impact: Screen reader and keyboard-only user accessibility
UX_02.testing: Navigate entire UI with keyboard only. Run NVDA/VoiceOver and verify all interactive elements announced.

UX_03.title: Add Skip-to-Content Link
UX_03.current_behavior: No skip-to-content link for keyboard users.

changes.action: Add skip link as first focusable element
changes.detail: Add `Skip to main content` as first child. Add `id='main-content'` to the main chat area.

UX_03.testing: Tab into page, verify skip link appears and works

UX_04.title: Add Light Mode / System Preference Detection
UX_04.current_behavior: Dark mode only. No light mode or `prefers-color-scheme` detection.

changes.action: Enable dark mode class strategy
changes.detail: Add `darkMode: 'class'` to tailwind config. This allows toggling dark mode via a class on the html element.

changes.action: Add theme detection and toggle
changes.detail: Add a `useEffect` that reads `prefers-color-scheme` media query and sets initial theme. Add a theme toggle button in the header. Store preference in localStorage. Apply 'dark' class to `<html>` element.

UX_04.impact: Users in bright environments can use light mode. Respects OS preference.
UX_04.testing: Toggle system dark mode, verify UI follows. Toggle manual switch, verify override works.

UX_05.title: Status Indicators Beyond Color-Only
UX_05.current_behavior: Status indicators use color only (green=running, red=error, yellow=pending). No text or icon alternatives.

changes.action: Add text labels and icons alongside colors
changes.detail: Add `aria-label` with status text. Add small text label below status dot: 'Running', 'Error', 'Done'. Add icons: ⏳ pending, ▶ running, ✓ done, ✕ error.

changes.action: Add text alongside connection status dot
changes.detail: Show 'Connected', 'Disconnected', 'Reconnecting' text next to the color indicator.

UX_05.impact: Color-blind users can determine status. Screen readers announce status.
UX_05.testing: Enable grayscale display filter, verify all statuses distinguishable

UX_06.title: Add prefers-reduced-motion Support
UX_06.current_behavior: Animations play regardless of user motion preferences.

changes.action: Add motion-safe wrapper to all animations
changes.detail: Replace `animate-spin` with `motion-safe:animate-spin`. Replace `animate-pulse` with `motion-safe:animate-pulse`. For CSS animations, add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { animation: none; }`. Tailwind's `motion-safe:` prefix handles this automatically.

UX_06.impact: Respects user accessibility preferences for motion sensitivity
UX_06.testing: Enable 'reduce motion' in OS, verify no animations play

UX_07.title: Responsive Model Usage Table
UX_07.current_behavior: Model usage grid unreadable on mobile. No responsive font sizing.

changes.action: Make model usage table responsive
changes.detail: On mobile (< 640px): switch from table to stacked card layout. Each model gets its own card with label: value pairs. Use `sm:table-cell hidden` / `sm:hidden block` pattern for responsive switching.

UX_07.testing: Resize browser to 375px width, verify all data visible and readable

UX_08.title: URL Routing for Shareable Links
UX_08.current_behavior: Single-page app with no URL routing. Can't share links to specific runs or conversations.

changes.action: Add URL hash-based routing
changes.detail: Use `window.location.hash` for lightweight routing: `#/chat`, `#/run/{id}`, `#/settings`. Listen for `hashchange` events. Update hash when navigating. On page load, parse hash and restore view.

UX_08.impact: Users can bookmark and share links to specific views
UX_08.testing: Navigate to run, copy URL, open in new tab, verify same view

UX_09.title: Add Breadcrumb Navigation
UX_09.current_behavior: No breadcrumb or back navigation between views.

changes.action: Add breadcrumb bar when viewing sub-views
changes.detail: When viewing run details or settings, show breadcrumb: `Chat > Run #123 > Worker A`. Clickable segments to navigate back.

UX_09.testing: Navigate into run detail, click breadcrumb to go back

UX_10.title: Improve Error Message Display
UX_10.current_behavior: ErrorMessage component is minimal (23 lines). Generic error display.

changes.action: Add error categorization and retry action
changes.detail: Categorize errors: network, auth, rate-limit, server. Show appropriate icon and message for each. Add 'Retry' button for retriable errors. Add 'Copy error' button for bug reports.

UX_10.impact: Users can understand and resolve errors faster
UX_10.testing: Trigger each error type, verify appropriate message and actions shown

UX_11.title: Add Loading Skeleton for Initial Page Load
UX_11.current_behavior: OrchestrationPane loads client-side only (SSR disabled). Shows blank space during hydration.

changes.action: Add skeleton placeholder during CSR hydration
changes.detail: Show a skeleton shimmer layout (matching final layout structure) while the dynamic import loads. Use the same skeleton pattern already used in ChatPane.

UX_11.impact: Eliminates layout shift during page load
UX_11.testing: Hard refresh page, verify skeleton appears then transitions smoothly to content

5_ARCHITECTURE._summary: Code organization, god file decomposition, dead code removal, dependency cleanup. 10 items.

ARCH_01.title: Decompose main-agent.ts God File (1,251 lines)
ARCH_01.current_behavior: MainAgent class handles 10+ responsibilities: message handling, tool execution, memory integration, streaming, error handling, checkpoint management, context assembly, conversation management.

changes.action: Extract into focused modules
changes.detail: Split into: (1) `main-agent.ts` — orchestration only (~200 lines). (2) `message-handler.ts` — user/assistant message processing. (3) `tool-executor.ts` — tool call dispatch and result handling. (4) `stream-manager.ts` — streaming response management. (5) `checkpoint-manager.ts` — checkpoint save/restore. (6) `context-builder.ts` — system prompt and context assembly. Each module exports a class or set of functions consumed by MainAgent.

ARCH_01.impact: Testable, maintainable components. Each module can be tested independently.
ARCH_01.testing: After decomposition, verify all existing functionality works. Each new module should have clear interface.

ARCH_02.title: Decompose gateway server.ts (861 lines)
ARCH_02.current_behavior: Single file handles HTTP server creation, all route registration, CORS, error handling, shutdown, and configuration.

changes.action: Extract route handlers into separate route files
changes.detail: Move inline route handlers to their respective route files: (1) Move /api/models handler to `routes/models.ts`. (2) Move /api/commands handler to `routes/commands.ts`. (3) Move /api/shutdown and /api/restart to `routes/lifecycle.ts`. (4) Keep server.ts as pure server setup + route registration (~200 lines). (5) Extract CORS handling to `middleware/cors.ts`.

ARCH_02.impact: Clean separation of concerns. Each route file independently testable.
ARCH_02.testing: All API endpoints respond identically after refactor

ARCH_03.title: Decompose setup.ts (1,631 lines)
ARCH_03.current_behavior: Largest file in codebase. Handles: interactive setup wizard, Docker management, config generation, dependency checking, service management, health verification.

changes.action: Split into focused setup modules
changes.detail: Extract: (1) `setup/wizard.ts` — interactive prompts and user input. (2) `setup/docker.ts` — Docker image management, container lifecycle. (3) `setup/config-generator.ts` — config.yaml generation and validation. (4) `setup/health-check.ts` — service health verification. (5) `setup/dependencies.ts` — system dependency checking (Node, Docker, etc). Keep `setup.ts` as orchestrator (~150 lines).

ARCH_03.impact: Each setup phase independently testable and maintainable
ARCH_03.testing: Run full setup wizard, verify identical behavior

ARCH_04.title: Type WebSocket Messages (Replace 'any')
ARCH_04.current_behavior: WebSocket message handlers type `msg` as `any`. No type safety for message parsing.

changes.action: Define typed message union
changes.detail: Create `packages/gateway/src/ws-types.ts` with discriminated union: `type WsMessage = TextMessage | DagDispatchedMessage | DagCompleteMessage | TokenUsageMessage | MemoryEventMessage | StatusMessage | ...`. Each type has `type` discriminant field. Use in send/receive handlers.

changes.action: Import and use typed messages
changes.detail: Import shared types (or duplicate if no shared package). Replace `any` with the discriminated union. Use type narrowing: `if (msg.type === 'text') { /* msg is TextMessage */ }`.

ARCH_04.impact: Compile-time safety for all WebSocket communication. Catches message format errors early.
ARCH_04.testing: TypeScript compilation should catch any message format mismatches

ARCH_05.title: Create Test Infrastructure
ARCH_05.current_behavior: ZERO test files in entire repository. No test runner configured.

changes.action: Set up Vitest across all packages
changes.detail: Install vitest as dev dependency. Create `vitest.config.ts` in each package. Add `test` script to each package.json. Create initial test files for critical paths: (1) `packages/gateway/src/__tests__/auth.test.ts` — auth validation. (2) `packages/hindsight/src/__tests__/similarity.test.ts` — relevance scoring. (3) `packages/core/src/memory/__tests__/retention-engine.test.ts` — quality scoring. (4) `packages/gateway/src/__tests__/routes.test.ts` — route auth guards. Target: 20 tests covering security-critical paths first.

ARCH_05.impact: Foundation for CI/CD, regression prevention, and confidence in refactoring
ARCH_05.testing: Run `pnpm test` across all packages, verify all pass

ARCH_06.title: Fix Silent Error Swallowing
ARCH_06.current_behavior: Fire-and-forget promises and checkpoint saves swallow errors with empty catch blocks or .catch(() => {}).

changes.action: Replace empty catch blocks with error logging
changes.detail: Search for `.catch(() => {})`, `.catch(() => undefined)`, `catch { }`, and `catch { /* ignore */ }` patterns. Replace with `.catch(err => logger.warn('Operation failed', { error: err.message, context: '<specific context>' }))`. For critical operations (checkpoint save, memory retain), add retry logic.

ARCH_06.impact: Errors become visible for debugging. Critical failures trigger retries.
ARCH_06.testing: Simulate failures (disk full, network down), verify errors logged

ARCH_07.title: Extract Shared Types Package
ARCH_07.current_behavior: Types duplicated across packages. WebSocket message types defined separately in gateway and web.

changes.action: Create @orionomega/shared-types package
changes.detail: Create `packages/shared-types/` with shared interfaces: WsMessage types, config schema, session types, memory types, API request/response types. Import from this package in core, gateway, and web packages.

ARCH_07.impact: Single source of truth for all cross-package types
ARCH_07.testing: TypeScript compilation across all packages succeeds with shared types

ARCH_08.title: Add API Versioning
ARCH_08.current_behavior: No API versioning. Routes are `/api/health`, not `/api/v1/health`.

changes.action: Add /v1/ prefix to all API routes
changes.detail: Prefix all routes with `/api/v1/`. Keep old routes as aliases for backward compatibility during transition. Add `API-Version: v1` response header.

ARCH_08.testing: Both `/api/health` and `/api/v1/health` should work during transition

ARCH_09.title: Reduce SettingsModal.tsx (1,061 lines)
ARCH_09.current_behavior: Single component handles all settings tabs, forms, and validation.

changes.action: Extract each tab into its own component
changes.detail: Split into: GeneralTab, ModelsTab, MemoryTab, OrchestrationTab components. SettingsModal becomes a thin shell (~100 lines) with tab switching and modal chrome.

ARCH_09.testing: All settings tabs function identically after split

ARCH_10.title: Add Error Boundaries to Key UI Sections
ARCH_10.current_behavior: Single ErrorBoundary exists (40 lines) but may not wrap all critical sections.

changes.action: Wrap each major section in ErrorBoundary
changes.detail: Add ErrorBoundary around: (1) OrchestrationPane (so DAG crash doesn't kill chat). (2) ChatPane message list (so bad message doesn't kill UI). (3) SettingsModal (so settings error doesn't kill app). Each boundary should show section-specific error message with retry button.

ARCH_10.impact: Partial failures don't crash entire application
ARCH_10.testing: Simulate component error, verify only that section shows error boundary

6_FEATURE_PARITY._summary: Ensuring CLI/TUI features work equivalently in WebUI. 7 items.

PARITY_01.title: File Output Viewing in WebUI
PARITY_01.current_behavior: CLI can view file contents (cat). WebUI only shows file path in tool results, can't view artifact contents.

changes.action: Add file content endpoint
changes.detail: Add `GET /api/files?path=<encoded_path>` endpoint (with auth + path traversal prevention). Returns file content with syntax highlighting hint based on extension. Limit to workspace directory only.

changes.action: Add 'View File' button for file paths
changes.detail: When tool result contains a file path, show 'View' button. On click, fetch file content from new endpoint. Display in modal with syntax highlighting.

PARITY_01.impact: Users can inspect generated files without switching to terminal
PARITY_01.testing: Run tool that creates file, click View, verify content displayed with highlighting

PARITY_02.title: Log Viewer in WebUI
PARITY_02.current_behavior: CLI has `orionomega logs` command. WebUI has no log viewing capability.

changes.action: Add log streaming endpoint and viewer component
changes.detail: Add `GET /api/logs?lines=100&follow=true` endpoint that reads from log files and optionally streams via SSE. Create `LogViewer.tsx` component with auto-scroll, log level filtering (info/warn/error), and search. Add as tab in OrchestrationPane or Settings.

PARITY_02.testing: Generate log entries, verify they appear in real-time in WebUI

PARITY_03.title: Doctor/Diagnostics in WebUI
PARITY_03.current_behavior: CLI has `orionomega doctor` command for system diagnostics. WebUI has no equivalent.

changes.action: Add diagnostics endpoint and UI
changes.detail: Add `GET /api/diagnostics` endpoint that runs same checks as CLI doctor: Node version, Docker status, Hindsight health, API key validity, disk space, memory usage. Return JSON results. Add 'Diagnostics' tab in Settings with pass/fail display for each check.

PARITY_03.testing: Run diagnostics with one service down, verify failure reported clearly

PARITY_04.title: Session Management in WebUI
PARITY_04.current_behavior: CLI can create and switch sessions. WebUI only uses 'default' session with no ability to create/switch.

changes.action: Add session selector UI
changes.detail: Add session dropdown in chat header. On click, show list of sessions from `GET /api/sessions`. 'New Session' button calls `POST /api/sessions`. Switching session reconnects WebSocket with new session ID. Show session name and creation date.

PARITY_04.impact: Users can manage multiple conversation contexts
PARITY_04.testing: Create 3 sessions, switch between them, verify messages are session-specific

PARITY_05.title: Update Progress Indicator
PARITY_05.current_behavior: Update command partially available but no progress indicator in WebUI.

changes.action: Add update progress display
changes.detail: When `POST /api/restart` or update is triggered, show progress bar or status messages in a modal. Stream update output via SSE.

PARITY_05.impact: Users know update status without switching to terminal

PARITY_06.title: sanitizeChatInput HTML Sanitization
PARITY_06.current_behavior: sanitizeChatInput strips some patterns but doesn't sanitize HTML injection.

changes.action: Add HTML entity encoding to sanitization
changes.detail: In `sanitizeChatInput()` at lines 82-93, add HTML entity encoding: replace `<` with `<`, `>` with `>`, `"` with `"`. This prevents HTML injection if content is ever rendered in non-React contexts.

PARITY_06.testing: Send message with `<script>alert(1)</script>`, verify entities escaped in storage

PARITY_07.title: WebSocket Client-Side Heartbeat
PARITY_07.current_behavior: No client-side heartbeat ping. Server ping timeout may go undetected by client.

changes.action: Add client-initiated ping/pong
changes.detail: Add 30-second heartbeat interval. Send `{ type: 'ping' }` message. If no pong received within 5 seconds, trigger reconnection. Clear interval on disconnect.

PARITY_07.impact: Faster detection of zombie WebSocket connections
PARITY_07.testing: Kill server process, verify client detects disconnect within 35 seconds

7_PERFORMANCE._summary: Optimization opportunities for response time, bundle size, and resource usage. 8 items.

PERF_01.title: Selective highlight.js Language Loading
PERF_01.current_behavior: highlight.js loads ALL grammars (~300KB). Most are never used.

changes.action: Switch to lowlight with selective language registration
changes.detail: Replace `highlight.js` import with `lowlight` (same API, tree-shakeable). Register only common languages: javascript, typescript, python, bash, json, yaml, html, css, sql, go, rust, java, markdown. Saves ~250KB bundle size.

PERF_01.testing: Verify syntax highlighting works for common languages. Measure bundle size before/after.

PERF_02.title: Memoize buildRenderItems in ChatPane
PERF_02.current_behavior: `buildRenderItems()` runs O(n) scan on every message change, even when only one message was added.

changes.action: Memoize with useMemo keyed on messages array reference
changes.detail: Wrap `buildRenderItems()` call in `useMemo(() => buildRenderItems(messages), [messages])`. Since Zustand returns new array reference only on actual changes, this prevents redundant recomputation.

PERF_02.impact: Eliminates redundant O(n) scans during rapid streaming
PERF_02.testing: Profile with React DevTools during streaming, verify no unnecessary re-renders

PERF_03.title: Optimize Image Attachment Storage
PERF_03.current_behavior: Image attachments stored as full base64 in localStorage. Large images consume quota rapidly.

changes.action: Resize images before storage and limit dimensions
changes.detail: Before base64 encoding: (1) Use Canvas API to resize images > 1920px to max 1920px width. (2) Compress JPEG to quality 0.8. (3) For PNG, convert to JPEG if no transparency. (4) Display thumbnail in chat, store reference to original on server.

PERF_03.testing: Attach 5MB image, verify stored size < 1MB, verify display quality acceptable

PERF_04.title: Redis-Backed Rate Limiting
PERF_04.current_behavior: In-memory rate limiting. Lost on server restart. Not shared across instances.

changes.action: Add optional Redis backend with in-memory fallback
changes.detail: If Redis URL configured in config, use Redis for rate limit counters. Otherwise fall back to current in-memory implementation. Use Redis MULTI/EXEC for atomic increment + TTL. Add to config: `rateLimit: { backend: 'memory' | 'redis', redisUrl?: string }`.

PERF_04.impact: Rate limits survive restarts and work across multiple instances
PERF_04.testing: With Redis: verify rate limit persists across restart. Without: verify fallback works.

PERF_05.title: Monitor Hindsight Memory Usage
PERF_05.current_behavior: Hindsight Python API consuming ~2GB (26% of 7.7GB RAM). No memory limits configured in Docker.

changes.action: Add Docker memory limit to Hindsight container
changes.detail: In docker run command, add `--memory=1g --memory-swap=1.5g` to cap Hindsight memory usage. Add `--restart=unless-stopped` for resilience.

PERF_05.impact: Prevents Hindsight from consuming all available RAM
PERF_05.testing: Restart Hindsight with limits, verify it operates within 1GB, verify OOM behavior graceful

PERF_06.title: Connection Pooling for Hindsight HTTP Client
PERF_06.current_behavior: Each HTTP request to Hindsight creates new connection. No connection pooling or keep-alive.

changes.action: Enable HTTP keep-alive and connection pooling
changes.detail: Use Node.js `http.Agent` with `{ keepAlive: true, maxSockets: 10 }`. Pass as agent option to all fetch/http calls. Reduces TCP handshake overhead for the 4N requests per turn.

PERF_06.testing: Measure recall latency before/after. Verify connection reuse via netstat.

PERF_07.title: History Reconciliation Robustness
PERF_07.current_behavior: processHistoryWhenHydrated uses 3-second timestamp bucketing for dedup. Misses messages on clock drift between client and server.

changes.action: Use message ID for dedup instead of timestamp
changes.detail: Replace timestamp bucketing with message ID comparison. Each message already has a unique ID. On history receive, filter out any messages whose IDs already exist in the local store. This is clock-independent.

PERF_07.testing: Disconnect, send 3 messages from another client, reconnect, verify exactly 3 new messages appear

PERF_08.title: Lazy Load OrchestrationPane Components
PERF_08.current_behavior: All orchestration components loaded upfront even if pane is collapsed.

changes.action: Lazy load tab contents
changes.detail: Use `React.lazy()` for DAGVisualization, MemoryFeed, and WorkflowSummary. Only load when their respective tab is selected. Show skeleton while loading.

PERF_08.impact: Faster initial page load when orchestration pane is collapsed
PERF_08.testing: Measure Time to Interactive with pane collapsed. Verify lazy loading on tab switch.

8_DOCUMENTATION._summary: README, API docs, deployment guides. 6 items.

DOC_01.title: API Reference Documentation
DOC_01.current_behavior: No OpenAPI/Swagger documentation for the gateway REST API.

changes.action: Generate OpenAPI 3.0 spec
changes.detail: Create `docs/api/openapi.yaml` documenting all gateway endpoints: request/response schemas, auth requirements, error codes. Serve via Swagger UI at `/api/docs` in development mode.

DOC_01.impact: Developers can understand and integrate with the API without reading source code
DOC_01.testing: Load Swagger UI, verify all endpoints documented with examples

DOC_02.title: Security Deployment Guide

changes.action: Create security deployment checklist
changes.detail: Create `docs/SECURITY-DEPLOYMENT.md` covering: (1) Enable auth and generate API key. (2) Configure TLS certificates. (3) Restrict network bindings. (4) Pin Docker image digests. (5) Configure rate limiting. (6) Set up audit logging. (7) Backup and encryption. (8) Monitoring and alerting.

DOC_02.impact: Users deploy securely by following checklist
DOC_02.testing: Follow guide on fresh install, verify all security measures active

DOC_03.title: Architecture Decision Records
DOC_03.current_behavior: No architecture decision records. Design rationale lives only in code comments.

changes.action: Create initial ADRs
changes.detail: Create `docs/adr/` directory with: ADR-001-monorepo-structure.md, ADR-002-memory-architecture.md, ADR-003-websocket-protocol.md, ADR-004-auth-model.md. Each ADR: Context, Decision, Consequences, Status.

DOC_03.impact: New contributors understand why decisions were made

DOC_04.title: Memory System Configuration Guide
DOC_04.current_behavior: Memory system has many configurable thresholds but no documentation on tuning them.

changes.action: Create memory tuning guide
changes.detail: Create `docs/MEMORY-TUNING.md` covering: budget tiers (low/mid/high), retention quality thresholds, bank management, recall diversity settings, compaction triggers, and recommendations for different use cases (solo dev, team, enterprise).

DOC_04.impact: Users can optimize memory behavior for their use case
DOC_04.testing: Follow guide recommendations, verify memory behavior changes as expected

DOC_05.title: WebSocket Protocol Documentation
DOC_05.current_behavior: WebSocket message format undocumented. Client developers must read source code.

changes.action: Document WebSocket protocol
changes.detail: Create `docs/WEBSOCKET-PROTOCOL.md` documenting: connection URL, auth handshake, all message types (ack, history, text, streaming, dag_*, memory_*, token_usage, ping/pong), message formats with JSON examples, reconnection behavior.

DOC_05.testing: Build simple WS client following only the docs, verify it works

DOC_06.title: Contributing Guide
DOC_06.current_behavior: No contributing guide for the open-source project.

changes.action: Create contributing guide
changes.detail: Create `CONTRIBUTING.md` covering: (1) Development setup (pnpm install, build, run). (2) Package structure overview. (3) Code style (TypeScript strict, no any). (4) PR process. (5) Testing requirements. (6) Security reporting process.

DOC_06.testing: New developer follows guide, successfully builds and runs project

phase_1_security_sprint.success_criteria: All API endpoints require auth. No exec() with unsanitized input. Hindsight bound to localhost. Security headers on web server.

phase_2_security_hardening.goal: TLS, token revocation, installer integrity, secure credential storage
phase_2_security_hardening.success_criteria: TLS configurable. Tokens revocable. Installer verifies integrity. API keys in env vars.

phase_3_memory_and_testing.goal: Fix memory token waste, improve recall, add test foundation
phase_3_memory_and_testing.success_criteria: 30%+ token cost reduction. Semantic recall improvement. 20+ critical path tests. Token usage visible in WebUI.

phase_4_webui_and_architecture.goal: Fix WebUI bugs, decompose god files, accessibility
phase_4_webui_and_architecture.success_criteria: No React key warnings. No DAG state drift. God files split. WCAG AA contrast. File viewing and session management in WebUI.

phase_5_polish.success_criteria: All MEDIUM items resolved. API docs published. Bundle size reduced. Feature parity gaps closed.

_cross_cutting_concerns.testing_strategy: Start with security-critical paths (auth, input validation, command execution). Expand to memory system (relevance scoring, retention decisions). Then WebUI (store actions, component rendering). Target 60% coverage by end of phase 5.
_cross_cutting_concerns.monitoring: Add structured logging (JSON format) with correlation IDs across gateway, WebSocket, and memory system. Track: request latency (p50/p95/p99), memory recall latency, token usage per session, error rates by category.
_cross_cutting_concerns.ci_cd: Set up GitHub Actions: lint (eslint + tsc), test (vitest), security audit (pnpm audit), build verification. Block merge on any failure.
_cross_cutting_concerns.backwards_compatibility: All API changes must be backwards compatible for at least 1 minor version. Use feature flags for behavioral changes. WebSocket protocol changes require version negotiation.
